docs.title: Technical Writer and Editor
docs.text:
I am a technical writer and editor with a background in macOS systems engineering and enterprise IT. My experience includes creating administrator documentation, user guides, knowledge base content, workflows, and process documentation for both technical teams and end users.

In addition to my documentation work in IT environments, I serve as a Technical Editor for Apache OpenOffice documentation, where I review and edit user guide content for clarity, consistency, usability, and technical accuracy. I have also completed Scriptorium’s Introduction to DITA course and maintain documentation samples in Markdown and GitHub-based formats.

This portfolio highlights selected writing and editing samples that demonstrate my ability to translate complex technical workflows into clear, structured, user-focused documentation.

docs.title: Managed macOS Onboarding Guide
docs.text: A task-based quick-start guide designed to help end users set up a company-managed macOS device in an enterprise environment. This project demonstrates clear onboarding workflows, authentication and security integration (MFA, secure access), and user-focused troubleshooting while reflecting real-world device management practices.

docs.title: Atari Computer User Guide
docs.text: A Markdown-based user guide created to apply technical writing principles to legacy computing documentation. This sample demonstrates step-by-step instructions, hardware setup guidance, and troubleshooting content for users familiar with modern systems but new to older platforms.

docs.title: Migrate User Administrative Tool Documentation
docs.text: Administrator-focused documentation for an AppleScript tool used to restore ownership and group permissions on a migrated macOS home folder after reimaging. This sample includes a polished README, revised prompts and warnings, improved maintainable comments, and supporting project documentation.

docs.title: Apache OpenOffice Technical Editing
docs.text: A technical editing sample based on published Apache OpenOffice user documentation. This project demonstrates editorial work in consistency, content editing, copyediting, and structural editing, supported by project overview, editing highlights, and before-and-after examples.

docs.title: About
docs.text:
I am a technical writer and editor with a background in macOS systems engineering and enterprise IT. My experience includes creating administrator documentation, user guides, knowledge base articles, workflows, playbooks, and process documentation for both technical teams and end users.

Throughout my IT career, I regularly translated technical processes into documentation that helped support teams, administrators, and end users work more effectively. I created internal how-to guides, getting-started materials, workflow documentation, and support content designed to improve consistency, reduce repeated questions, and make technical processes easier to understand and maintain. That experience led me to focus more directly on technical writing and editing.

I currently serve as a Technical Editor for Apache OpenOffice documentation, where I review and edit user guide content for clarity, consistency, usability, and technical accuracy. I have also completed Scriptorium’s Introduction to DITA course and maintain documentation samples in Markdown and GitHub-based formats as part of my portfolio.

My technical background includes macOS, JAMF Pro, endpoint management, enterprise support environments, and security-focused systems work. That hands-on experience helps me understand the systems I document and translate complex technical workflows into clear, user-focused content.

I am especially interested in work involving:

  • Technical writing
  • Technical editing
  • User and administrator documentation
  • Knowledge base content
  • IT and endpoint documentation
  • Process and workflow documentation

docs.text: Technical writer and editor with a background in macOS systems engineering and enterprise IT. Experienced in developing user-focused documentation, administrator guides, knowledge base content, workflows, playbooks, and process documentation for internal teams and end users. Combines strong editing and documentation skills with hands-on expertise in macOS, endpoint management, JAMF Pro, and security-focused enterprise environments.

docs.title: Technical Editor — Apache Software Foundation
docs.text: Jul 2024–Present - Serve as technical editor for OpenOffice user documentation, with primary responsibility for Calc user guides. - Reviewed and edited the complete Calc 4.1, Draw 4.1, and Impress 4.1 user guides for clarity, consistency, usability, and technical accuracy.

docs.title: Aether Project Generalist — Outlier AI
docs.text: Dec 2025–Present - Evaluate and improve AI-generated content for grammar, clarity, coherence, and communication quality. - Apply editorial judgment to improve readability, consistency, and overall language performance.

docs.title: macOS Systems Engineer — BlackRock, Inc.
docs.text: Mar 2018–Mar 2024 - Created and maintained internal technical documentation, including how-to guides, knowledge base content, engineering workflows, and evaluations of new technologies. - Wrote getting-started documentation for end users and process documentation for support and engineering teams. - Translated complex macOS administration and security procedures into clear, reusable documentation for technical and operational audiences.

docs.title: Migrate User Administrative Tool Documentation
docs.text: This sample documents an AppleScript-based administrative tool for restoring ownership and group permissions to a migrated user's home folder after a Mac has been reimaged. The project includes a polished administrator README and a revised script with clearer prompts, improved warnings, maintainable comments, and more administrator-focused messaging.

docs.title: Apache OpenOffice Technical Editing
docs.text: This sample highlights my technical editing work on Apache OpenOffice user documentation. The project demonstrates my ability to review and improve large documentation sets for clarity, consistency, usability, structure, and technical accuracy.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
My editing work focused on major OpenOffice user guides, including:

  • Calc 4.1 User Guide
  • Draw 4.1 User Guide
  • Impress 4.1 User Guide

These projects required careful editorial review of end-user documentation to improve terminology, readability, organization, and overall documentation quality.

docs.title: My Role
docs.text: I served as a technical editor for Apache OpenOffice user documentation. My work included reviewing and editing content for clarity, consistency, readability, usability, and technical accuracy across multiple user guides.

docs.title: Editing Focus Areas
docs.text:
This sample demonstrates several forms of editing, including:

  • Consistency editing — standardizing terminology, usage, and formatting
  • Copyediting — improving grammar, punctuation, sentence structure, and readability
  • Content editing — clarifying meaning, improving completeness, and refining wording
  • Structural editing — improving organization, flow, headings, and presentation

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: This sample is intended to demonstrate technical editing in a documentation context rather than original authoring alone. It reflects my ability to evaluate existing documentation, identify opportunities for improvement, and revise content to better support clarity, consistency, and reader comprehension.
